H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: H-OS-L1 (Pebble) recorded n=95, mean=7.15, std=1.78, confidence=strong. Positive responses reach 66 % and the distribution clusters around 7‑9. Qualitative data (n=29) emphasize noticeable improvement, balanced rhythm, and typical 10‑minute settle times; listeners include drivers and casual listeners. YouTube reach is highest (30 views) but retention metrics are unavailable. AutoGluon predicts 6.859 for overstimulation, slightly below the observed mean, confirming efficacy despite limited engagement data.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: This over‑stimulation relief is consistently praised and works well for drivers and commuters.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: H-OS-L2 (Rainfall) has n=51, mean=6.92, std=1.73, confidence=strong. Positive rate is 65 % with a compact score distribution. Qualitative feedback (n=21) shows noticeable improvement and mixed music opinions; settle times favor 10 min. No adviser traffic. YouTube views are 5 with no retention data. AutoGluon prediction (6.859) aligns closely with observed performance.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Listeners notice a steady drop in overwhelm, though music opinions vary.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: H-OS-L3 (Glacier) reports n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful. Positive responses are 64 % and the distribution is smooth across 6‑9. Qualitative responses (n=23) note fast settle (5‑10 min) and balanced rhythm; two adviser‑driven listeners suggest targeted distribution. YouTube views are 8, retention absent. AutoGluon predicts 6.859, modestly lower than observed.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: People report quick calming, making it a reliable choice for busy moments.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: H-AX-L1 (Meadow) recorded n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93, confidence=strong. The outcome distribution leans positive (63 % positive) with a modest tail toward high scores. Qualitative feedback (n=23) shows most listeners self‑administered, reporting moderate to significant improvement and a typical 10‑minute settle time; rhythm was described as balanced. YouTube reach is minimal (7 views) with no retention data, indicating undiscovered efficacy. AutoGluon predicts 7.838 for the anxiety zone, modestly above the observed mean, suggesting latent upside. Overall the signal is robust but limited by discoverability.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Listeners often feel noticeably calmer after about ten minutes, even though few discover it online.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: H-AX-L2 (Cottage) achieved n=48, mean=6.94, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful. Positive responses reach 67 %, with a tighter score cluster around 7‑8. Qualitative responses (n=20) note mixed music opinions and settle times ranging 5‑20 min, hinting at variable entrainment speed. All traffic is unknown‑source, limiting routing insight. No YouTube metrics are available. AutoGluon does not flag a cross‑bucket shift, aligning with the observed mean.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: The protocol gives a steady sense of relief, though some listeners find the music just okay.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: H-AX-L3 (Valley) reports n=36, mean=7.08, std=1.89, confidence=meaningful. Positive rate is 61 % and the distribution shows a long tail to high scores (five 10s). Qualitative data (n=20) emphasize very settling rhythm and 10‑minute settle times; a minority reported no effect. All listeners are unknown‑source. No YouTube data. AutoGluon predicts 7.838 (anxiety), matching the observed performance.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: Most people notice a calming effect, especially if they stay with the whole session.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: H-CR-L1 (Anchor) has n=43, mean=6.35, std=1.70, confidence=meaningful, reflecting expected lower scores for acute distress. Positive responses are 53 %, yet qualitative input (n=24) highlights significant improvement for many and rapid settle (5 min or less). Source is entirely unknown. YouTube views are low (6). AutoGluon predicts 7.955 for the crisis zone, notably higher than observed, suggesting a possible routing or severity mismatch.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: In acute distress, users report quick calming, though overall scores stay modest.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: H-CR-L2 (Harbor) reports n=33, mean=6.52, std=1.70, confidence=meaningful. Positive rate rises to 55 % with a concentration of scores 7‑8. Qualitative feedback (n=16) notes mixed music opinion but strong rhythm settling; contexts include work and sleep. No YouTube data (4 views). AutoGluon predicts 7.955 for crisis, again above the observed mean, indicating potential for better targeting.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: A gentle improvement is common, with many finding the rhythm soothing during work or rest.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: H-CR-L3 (Horizon) shows n=27, mean=6.52, std=1.91, confidence=meaningful. The distribution is bimodal, with peaks at low (2‑4) and high (9‑10) scores, flagging subgroup effects. Qualitative responses (n=19) report immediate settlement for many but also four negative music opinions. YouTube views are 5. AutoGluon predicts 7.955 for crisis, diverging from the observed mean and supporting a routing reconsideration.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Some listeners love it right away, while others feel it doesn’t help, hinting at different needs.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: H-SL-L1 (Lantern) recorded n=54, mean=7.19, std=1.86, confidence=strong. Positive rate 65 % with a broad score spread; 10 % of listeners gave the maximum score. Qualitative data (n=15) are dominated by self‑listeners, reporting immediate to 5‑minute settlement and balanced rhythm; music was largely liked. YouTube views are 4, no retention. AutoGluon predicts 7.539 for sleep, slightly below the observed mean. Notable CROSS‑RUN DRIFT (sim=0.605) suggests analytical framing shift.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: Sleep‑focused listeners often feel better quickly, but the protocol’s effect seems to be shifting over time.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: H-SL-L2 (Hammock) achieved n=45, mean=7.09, std=1.55, confidence=meaningful. Positive rate peaks at 67 % with tight clustering around 7‑8. Qualitative feedback (n=15) shows a client‑patient heavy listener mix, neutral music opinions, and some rhythm rated as too busy. No adviser traffic. YouTube views are 5. AutoGluon prediction (7.539) matches observed mean, reinforcing consistency.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Most report a gentle improvement before sleep, though a few find the rhythm a bit busy.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: H-SL-L3 (Midnight) reports n=28, mean=7.07, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful. Positive responses are 64 % with a balanced distribution. Qualitative input (n=22) includes self‑listeners and caregivers, noting 5‑minute settle times and balanced rhythm; music reception is mixed but generally positive. YouTube views are 3. AutoGluon predicts 7.539, aligning with observed performance.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: Listeners generally settle into sleep more easily after a short period.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: T-FC-L1 (Compass) has n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82, confidence=meaningful. Positive rate is 56 % with a spread toward lower scores; rhythm feedback includes a notable “slightly busy” theme. Qualitative responses (n=23) highlight noticeable improvement but also busy rhythm concerns. YouTube views are 5. AutoGluon predicts 7.728 for focus, substantially above observed, suggesting the protocol may benefit from rhythm refinement.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Focus improves for many, but the rhythm can feel a little too busy for some.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: T-FC-L2 (Waypoint) recorded n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.60, confidence=meaningful. Positive rate climbs to 66 % with a clear shift upward from L1. Qualitative input (n=17) shows caregiver contexts and mixed music opinions, but balanced rhythm overall. YouTube views are 4. AutoGluon prediction (7.728) aligns with the observed uplift, supporting its current design.
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Most users experience clearer focus and enjoy the music, despite mixed opinions.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: T-FC-L3 (Summit) reported n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97, confidence=meaningful. Positive rate peaks at 71 % with a notable ten‑score cluster. Qualitative feedback (n=29) emphasizes significant improvement and strong music love. YouTube views are 15. AutoGluon predicts 7.728 for focus, closely matching observed high efficacy, making it a candidate for broader dissemination.
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: This is the top focus protocol, with many reporting strong clarity even during chores.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: T-CF-L1 (Ember) has n=34, mean=6.74, std=1.73, confidence=meaningful. Positive rate is 53 % and the distribution leans toward mid‑range scores. Qualitative responses (n=21) emphasize slight improvement, rapid settlement, and strong music liking. No YouTube data (2 views). AutoGluon does not list a cross‑bucket shift, indicating alignment with observed modest efficacy.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: Creative flow feels only slightly boosted, and many notice only a mild lift.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: T-CF-L2 (Current) recorded n=24, mean=6.79, std=1.41, confidence=meaningful. Positive rate rises to 58 % with tighter variance. Qualitative feedback (n=15) reports several significant improvements and high love for the music. YouTube views are 2, retention not yet available. AutoGluon prediction aligns closely with observed mean.
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: A modest creative spark appears, with several users loving the music.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: T-CF-L3 (Aurora) achieved n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32, confidence=developing. Positive responses reach 67 % and the distribution is compact with no low outliers. Qualitative data (n=25) note noticeable and significant improvements, balanced rhythm, and mixed activity contexts. YouTube views are 7. AutoGluon does not flag a cross‑bucket anomaly, confirming strong performance despite small sample.
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: Creative professionals report a solid boost in flow, especially after a brief settle.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: T-MB-L1 (Stone) achieved n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34, confidence=meaningful. Positive responses are 64 % with tight clustering. Qualitative data (n=23) show therapy‑session dominance and long settle times (up to 20 min). YouTube views are 3. AutoGluon predicts 7.693 for maintenance, slightly above observed, suggesting modest room for improvement.
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Maintenance feels steady, though some need longer time to notice the effect.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: T-MB-L2 (Axis) recorded n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03, confidence=meaningful. Positive rate is 59 % but variance is highest in the maintenance set. Qualitative feedback (n=16) notes mixed music opinions and rhythm flagged as slightly busy. YouTube views are 4. AutoGluon prediction (7.693) exceeds observed mean, indicating potential under‑performance.
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: Results vary; a few find the rhythm slightly busy, but most notice a subtle balance.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: T-MB-L3 (Orbit) has n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73, confidence=developing. Positive rate reaches 71 % but sample is small. Qualitative responses (n=19) include four “did NOT settle” reports, hinting at entrainment failure for some. YouTube views are 3. AutoGluon predicts 7.693 for maintenance, above observed, flagging a development opportunity.
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: Most feel better, but a handful didn’t settle, indicating the ascent may be too fast for them.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: X-ME-L1 (Breeze) recorded n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, confidence=meaningful. Positive rate is 54 % with balanced rhythm feedback. Qualitative data (n=22) emphasize caregiver contexts and strong music love. YouTube views are 8. AutoGluon predicts a low‑intensity score of 8.230, notably higher than observed, suggesting this protocol could be more effective for low‑affect listeners if tailored.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: Mood lifts are evident for caregivers, though the protocol may be under‑used online.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: X-ME-L2 (Sunrise) achieved n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71, confidence=meaningful. Positive rate climbs to 64 % with notable significant improvements. Qualitative feedback (n=15) shows therapy contexts and strong music love. YouTube views are 8. AutoGluon prediction (8.230) again exceeds observed, highlighting possible under‑utilization.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: Consistent mood improvements are reported, especially in therapy contexts.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: X-ME-L3 (Updraft) has n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, confidence=developing. Positive rate is 56 % with moderate variance. Qualitative responses (n=25) note mixed music opinions and balanced rhythm. YouTube views are 4. AutoGluon predicts 8.230, suggesting room for improvement in engagement or audience targeting.
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: Moderate mood benefits appear, but engagement could be stronger.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: X-MD-L1 (Footpath) recorded n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01, confidence=meaningful. Positive rate is 45 % and variance is high, suggesting divergent listener experiences. Qualitative data (n=17) note noticeable improvements but also mixed music love. YouTube views are 5. AutoGluon predicts 7.876 for motivation, substantially above observed, pointing to a possible ascent‑gradient issue.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Motivation gains are modest and varied; the ascent may feel too steep for some listeners.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: X-MD-L2 (Trail) achieved n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63, confidence=developing. Positive rate is 43 % with modest variance. Qualitative feedback (n=21) shows noticeable improvement and balanced rhythm, but lower music enthusiasm. YouTube views are 7. AutoGluon predicts 7.876, again above observed, suggesting a need to temper the ascent profile.
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: Listeners see slight motivation lifts, but overall impact remains limited.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: X-MD-L3 (Switchback) has n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38, confidence=developing. Positive rate rises to 62 % but variance is highest in the motivation set. Qualitative input (n=17) reports moderate improvement and strong music love. YouTube views are 7. AutoGluon predicts 7.876, aligning with the higher observed mean, indicating the ascent may work for a subset.
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: Motivation improves for a good portion of users, especially those who love the music.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: X-MC-L1 (Cavern) recorded n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful. Positive rate is 54 % with two reports of worsening outcomes. Qualitative data (n=25) show mixed music love and caregiver contexts. YouTube views are 8. AutoGluon predicts 7.538 for mind clarity, above observed, suggesting a possible subpopulation benefit.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Mind clarity outcomes are mixed; a few users even felt worse, suggesting it isn’t for everyone.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: X-MC-L2 (Starlight) achieved n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64, confidence=developing. Positive rate is 56 % with several significant improvements. Qualitative feedback (n=20) highlights very settling rhythm and strong music love. YouTube views are 12. AutoGluon predicts 7.538, modestly higher than observed, indicating latent upside.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: Many notice clearer thinking, especially when the rhythm feels very settling.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: X-MC-L3 (Cosmos) has n=10, mean=5.70, std=2.45, confidence=developing. Positive rate drops to 50 % and the distribution is bimodal with gaps at mid‑range scores. Qualitative responses (n=17) include several neutral music opinions and two “worse” reports, flagging a subgroup mismatch. YouTube views are 8. AutoGluon predicts 7.538, markedly higher, reinforcing the need to investigate population segmentation.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: Results split between strong benefit and no effect, pointing to a specific audience need.

sphere_commentary.overview:
The dataset shows robust efficacy signals across all series, with HEALING leading in sample size and consistency, THRIVING matching its mean but excelling in focus protocols, and TRANSFORMING displaying the lowest mean and highest variance.
• HEALING (n=574) achieves the highest mean (6.98) and tightest overall spread.
• THRIVING (n=245) mirrors the overall mean (6.96) yet its top protocol (T-FC-L3) reaches 7.63.
• TRANSFORMING (n=181) trails with mean 6.59 and notable variability, especially in X-MC-L3.
• Feature importance highlights engagement metrics (avg_view_percentage, avg_view_duration) as strongest predictors of outcome.
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
Efficacy patterns align with each series' design intent while revealing subtleties for refinement.
• HEALING shows the most stable responses; over‑stimulation and sleep entries exceed 7.0 mean.
• THRIVING’s HOLD protocols, particularly Focus & Clarity, deliver the highest observed scores.
• TRANSFORMING’s ASCENT arcs yield broader spread; Mood & Energy outperform Motivation & Drive.
• AutoGluon cross‑bucket suggestions frequently route TRANSFORMING Mood protocols to HEALING zones.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
A few protocols diverge from expected patterns, flagging potential subpopulation effects or data‑drift issues.
• X-MC-L3 (mean=5.70, std=2.45) shows a bimodal distribution and low positive rate.
• H‑SL‑L1 exhibits the greatest CROSS‑RUN DRIFT (sim=0.605), suggesting shifting analytical framing.
• H‑CR‑L3 displays a bimodal score pattern for a crisis entry, indicating possible subgroup divergence.
• Low YouTube view counts for high‑scoring protocols (e.g., H‑AX‑L1) hint at discoverability gaps.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
The data points to several concrete avenues for protocol iteration.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X-MC-L3 – investigate population split; consider L2/L3 variants targeting responsive subgroups.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] H-AX-L1 – improve YouTube metadata and adviser routing to boost reach for a high‑efficacy protocol.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] H-OS-L1 – test adviser‑driven vs organic routing to isolate efficacy from source bias.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] T-FC-L3 – evaluate cross‑bucket routing implications; refine hold duration to maximize focus outcomes.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] H-SL-L1 – reassess drift; run replication with controlled volume to confirm stability.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X-MD-L1 – examine ascent gradient; prototype a HOLD variant to reduce variance.
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding signals reveal both evolution and coherence across protocols.
• CROSS‑RUN DRIFT: 22 protocols show notable drift (sim < 0.75), with H‑SL‑L1 most extreme at 0.605.
• CROSS‑PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: Crisis protocols (H‑CR‑L1 ↔ H‑CR‑L3) and Sleep protocols (H‑SL‑L1 ↔ H‑SL‑L3) form tight clusters; Creative Flow (T‑CF‑L1 ↔ T‑CF‑L3) also clusters strongly.
• Confidence: embedding dataset is sufficient for high‑level patterns but limited for fine‑grained subgroup analysis.

by_protocol.X-MC-L2: X-MC-L2 (Starlight) achieved n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64, confidence=developing. Positive rate is 56 % with several significant improvements. Qualitative feedback (n=20) highlights very settling rhythm and strong music love. YouTube views are 12. AutoGluon predicts 7.538, modestly higher than observed, indicating latent upside.
by_protocol.X-MC-L3: X-MC-L3 (Cosmos) has n=10, mean=5.70, std=2.45, confidence=developing. Positive rate drops to 50 % and the distribution is bimodal with gaps at mid‑range scores. Qualitative responses (n=17) include several neutral music opinions and two “worse” reports, flagging a subgroup mismatch. YouTube views are 8. AutoGluon predicts 7.538, markedly higher, reinforcing the need to investigate population segmentation.
by_protocol.X-MD-L1: X-MD-L1 (Footpath) recorded n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01, confidence=meaningful. Positive rate is 45 % and variance is high, suggesting divergent listener experiences. Qualitative data (n=17) note noticeable improvements but also mixed music love. YouTube views are 5. AutoGluon predicts 7.876 for motivation, substantially above observed, pointing to a possible ascent‑gradient issue.
by_protocol.X-MD-L2: X-MD-L2 (Trail) achieved n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63, confidence=developing. Positive rate is 43 % with modest variance. Qualitative feedback (n=21) shows noticeable improvement and balanced rhythm, but lower music enthusiasm. YouTube views are 7. AutoGluon predicts 7.876, again above observed, suggesting a need to temper the ascent profile.
by_protocol.X-MD-L3: X-MD-L3 (Switchback) has n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38, confidence=developing. Positive rate rises to 62 % but variance is highest in the motivation set. Qualitative input (n=17) reports moderate improvement and strong music love. YouTube views are 7. AutoGluon predicts 7.876, aligning with the higher observed mean, indicating the ascent may work for a subset.
by_protocol.X-ME-L1: X-ME-L1 (Breeze) recorded n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, confidence=meaningful. Positive rate is 54 % with balanced rhythm feedback. Qualitative data (n=22) emphasize caregiver contexts and strong music love. YouTube views are 8. AutoGluon predicts a low‑intensity score of 8.230, notably higher than observed, suggesting this protocol could be more effective for low‑affect listeners if tailored.
by_protocol.X-ME-L2: X-ME-L2 (Sunrise) achieved n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71, confidence=meaningful. Positive rate climbs to 64 % with notable significant improvements. Qualitative feedback (n=15) shows therapy contexts and strong music love. YouTube views are 8. AutoGluon prediction (8.230) again exceeds observed, highlighting possible under‑utilization.
by_protocol.X-ME-L3: X-ME-L3 (Updraft) has n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, confidence=developing. Positive rate is 56 % with moderate variance. Qualitative responses (n=25) note mixed music opinions and balanced rhythm. YouTube views are 4. AutoGluon predicts 8.230, suggesting room for improvement in engagement or audience targeting.

by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L1: Listeners often feel noticeably calmer after about ten minutes, even though few discover it online.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L2: The protocol gives a steady sense of relief, though some listeners find the music just okay.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L3: Most people notice a calming effect, especially if they stay with the whole session.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L1: In acute distress, users report quick calming, though overall scores stay modest.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L2: A gentle improvement is common, with many finding the rhythm soothing during work or rest.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L3: Some listeners love it right away, while others feel it doesn’t help, hinting at different needs.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L1: This over‑stimulation relief is consistently praised and works well for drivers and commuters.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L2: Listeners notice a steady drop in overwhelm, though music opinions vary.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L3: People report quick calming, making it a reliable choice for busy moments.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L1: Sleep‑focused listeners often feel better quickly, but the protocol’s effect seems to be shifting over time.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L2: Most report a gentle improvement before sleep, though a few find the rhythm a bit busy.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L3: Listeners generally settle into sleep more easily after a short period.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L1: Creative flow feels only slightly boosted, and many notice only a mild lift.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L2: A modest creative spark appears, with several users loving the music.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L3: Creative professionals report a solid boost in flow, especially after a brief settle.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L1: Focus improves for many, but the rhythm can feel a little too busy for some.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L2: Most users experience clearer focus and enjoy the music, despite mixed opinions.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L3: This is the top focus protocol, with many reporting strong clarity even during chores.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L1: Maintenance feels steady, though some need longer time to notice the effect.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L2: Results vary; a few find the rhythm slightly busy, but most notice a subtle balance.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L3: Most feel better, but a handful didn’t settle, indicating the ascent may be too fast for them.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L1: Mind clarity outcomes are mixed; a few users even felt worse, suggesting it isn’t for everyone.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L2: Many notice clearer thinking, especially when the rhythm feels very settling.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L3: Results split between strong benefit and no effect, pointing to a specific audience need.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L1: Motivation gains are modest and varied; the ascent may feel too steep for some listeners.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L2: Listeners see slight motivation lifts, but overall impact remains limited.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L3: Motivation improves for a good portion of users, especially those who love the music.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L1: Mood lifts are evident for caregivers, though the protocol may be under‑used online.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L2: Consistent mood improvements are reported, especially in therapy contexts.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L3: Moderate mood benefits appear, but engagement could be stronger.
